name: Herb-Oatmeal Bread (Clay-pot)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the yeast, honey, and salt with 1 cup lukewarm (not hot) water. Set aside.
2. Heat 1 cup water to boiling, then add oatmeal and sour cream. Simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, remove from heat and cool to lukewarm.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine whole-wheat flour, seeds, and herbs. Add the oatmeal and yeast mixtures and beat until well blended.
4. Add beaten egg and melted butter, then beat several times again.
5. Add pine nuts, then add ¾ cup of all-purpose flour, one cup at a time, beating between each addition until a fairly stiff dough is developed.
6. Spread remaining all-purpose flour on a board and knead the dough thoroughly (about a dozen times) before transferring it to a large, well-greased bowl. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise in a warm place until doubled its original bulk (about 1 hour).
7. Just before the end of the rising time, presoak 2 clay pots top and bottom in hot water for 15 minutes.
8. Place the risen dough on a floured board, divide into 2 loaves, and shape.
9. Before placing the loaves in the pots, put a small piece of aluminum foil large enough to cover the bottom of each loaf in the pots' bottoms.
10. Place the dough inside the pots, cover, and allow to rise until almost doubled in bulk (about 45 minutes).
11. Wrap the pots and place them in a cold oven preheated to 480 degrees.
12. Bake for 40 minutes. If necessary, remove lid for the last 5 minutes of baking to achieve a golden brown crust.
Source: The Clay-Pot Cookbook by Georgia MacLeod and Grover Sales
Note: This is one of the best breads I've made in a long time. High loaf with light texture and crisp crust on the inside. Pine nuts add minimal flavor and could be omitted. Had trouble with sticking; my clay pot has a semi-glazed bottom, which I lightly greased with shortening, but it still stuck in some spots.
